task: Migrate ~40 independent Node modules from CommonJS to ESM in parallel, then land a single cutover (package type:module + build/test config), without regressing any module's tests
loop_altitude_rationale: ~40 largely-independent modules can be converted concurrently with no shared-write conflict (each module + its tests is a disjoint surface), and the scale makes fan-out a real speedup over sequential. Parallelism = concurrent codex exec processes in separate git worktrees coordinating via an on-disk ledger — large, not medium.

selection_log.why: per-module done is machine-decidable: that module's unit tests pass under ESM AND a residual scan (AST/grep for require/module.exports/__dirname) finds zero CJS constructs in it; final cutover is decidable by the whole suite + build exit code. A runnable check answers done ⇒ loop.

seam A: characterize hands a GREEN whole-suite baseline (tag `cjs-baseline`) + a per-module manifest to the conversion shards. seam B: every conversion shard hands a converted+green module set to cutover, which flips package.json/config and runs the whole suite. Each conversion shard's check runs without cutover existing; cutover consumes the converted modules unchanged; shards edit disjoint files ⇒ boundaries pass the seam test.

characterize enumerates the module graph + pins the baseline (explore_narrow); each shard is a mechanical, retryable per-module transform gated by tests + a residual scan (retry); cutover is a one-shot risky flip needing mid-flight correction across the whole build (plan_execute_verify).

the migration is reversible per worktree (discard a bad worktree) and each shard's check discriminates (tests + residual scan), so a wrong shard is caught at its gate ⇒ human reviews at the merge/cutover gates, not each per-module iteration. cutover is the one high-blast step and is reviewed before it lands.

the ~40 modules are largely independent — there exist many stage-pairs (the conversion shards) with NO dependency path between them that benefit from running as separate concurrent agents with no shared-write conflict. Realized as concurrent codex exec processes, each in its own git worktree, coordinating only through a shared on-disk ledger; a final gate stage merges the worktrees and runs the whole-suite check.

each conversion shard cap 8, on_failure restart (discard the shard's worktree and re-derive that module set from the manifest — a half-converted module is archaeology, a clean re-transform is faster); cutover cap 3 loopback->characterize if the merge reveals a mis-pinned baseline; outer escalate on an unresolvable cross-module import cycle that blocks independent conversion.

per-module tests + residual scan are fast/cheap, so each shard runs many short passes (one module per pass, cap 8) with near-free feedback. cutover is the one completeness-first exception (cap 3, thorough whole-suite+build check) since re-entering a whole-repo flip is expensive — recorded as a per-stage cadence exception.

definition_of_done.goal: the whole test suite passes on the untouched CJS baseline, is pinned as git tag `cjs-baseline`, and a per-module manifest (.loop/modules.json listing each module + its test command) is written

feedback_signal.check: npm test && test -s .loop/modules.json && git tag -f cjs-baseline
feedback_signal.falsifiable_when: the baseline suite is red, or .loop/modules.json is empty/missing — the check exits non-zero
feedback_signal.passing_but_wrong: the manifest lists only a subset of modules so untracked modules are silently never converted -> ruled out: the manifest is generated by scanning for every file containing a require()/module.exports and the count is asserted against `git ls-files '*.js' | xargs grep -l` so a short manifest fails

failure: baseline suite cannot be made green on the untouched tree (environment broken)

definition_of_done.goal: every module assigned to shard A is ESM (import/export) with its unit tests green under ESM and a residual scan finding zero CJS constructs in shard A's files

feedback_signal.check: npm run test:shard -- a && node scripts/cjs-residual-scan.mjs --shard a --expect-zero
feedback_signal.falsifiable_when: a shard-A module still contains require()/module.exports/__dirname/__filename, or a shard-A test fails under ESM -> non-zero exit
feedback_signal.passing_but_wrong: the residual scan greps only `require(` and misses `module.exports`, dynamic require, __dirname, or `import x = require()` -> ruled out: the scan is AST-based and enumerates the whole CJS class (require call, module.exports/exports assignment, __dirname/__filename, createRequire) not a single token

failure: a shard-A module cannot be converted without touching another shard's files (hidden coupling)

definition_of_done.goal: every module assigned to shard B is ESM with its unit tests green under ESM and a residual scan finding zero CJS constructs in shard B's files

feedback_signal.check: npm run test:shard -- b && node scripts/cjs-residual-scan.mjs --shard b --expect-zero
feedback_signal.falsifiable_when: a shard-B module still contains a CJS construct, or a shard-B test fails under ESM -> non-zero exit
feedback_signal.passing_but_wrong: a module is 'converted' but its test was never run under the ESM loader (still executed via the CJS jest transform) -> ruled out: test:shard runs node with --experimental-vm-modules / the ESM test runner so a module that only works under CJS fails

failure: a shard-B module cannot be converted without touching another shard's files (hidden coupling)

definition_of_done.goal: every module assigned to shard C is ESM with its unit tests green under ESM and a residual scan finding zero CJS constructs in shard C's files

feedback_signal.check: npm run test:shard -- c && node scripts/cjs-residual-scan.mjs --shard c --expect-zero
feedback_signal.falsifiable_when: a shard-C module still contains a CJS construct, or a shard-C test fails under ESM -> non-zero exit
feedback_signal.passing_but_wrong: an import path drops the required .js extension (ESM needs explicit extensions) but the stale CJS resolver still finds it during the shard test -> ruled out: the shard test runs under the pure ESM loader where extensionless relative imports throw ERR_MODULE_NOT_FOUND

failure: a shard-C module cannot be converted without touching another shard's files (hidden coupling)

definition_of_done.goal: all shard worktrees are merged, package.json has "type":"module", build/test config is ESM, and the WHOLE suite + build pass with a repo-wide residual scan finding zero CJS constructs

feedback_signal.check: npm run build && npm test && node scripts/cjs-residual-scan.mjs --all --expect-zero
feedback_signal.falsifiable_when: the merged tree fails the build, any test fails under type:module, or the repo-wide residual scan finds a leftover CJS construct anywhere -> non-zero exit
feedback_signal.passing_but_wrong: two shards each converted a shared helper differently and the merge silently kept one, breaking the other's callers -> ruled out: cutover runs the WHOLE suite (not per-shard) so a cross-shard breakage surfaces, and the repo-wide residual scan covers files no shard owned

failure: merged tree cannot build under type:module without reverting a shard
a cross-shard conflict cannot be resolved without re-sharding

maker_checker.scope: a fresh-context reviewer sees only the merged diff + the whole-suite check + the repo-wide residual scan; flags any cross-shard regression, an extensionless import, or a module missing from the manifest — not per-module style

planner.mandate: enumerate the module graph, shard the ~40 modules into disjoint independent sets, write the manifest + contract; never convert a module

generator.mandate: in each shard worktree, convert that shard's modules to ESM and keep their tests green; may NOT touch another shard's files, weaken a test, or grade its own shard

evaluator.mandate: a fresh read-only codex exec that never saw any shard's conversion; given only the merged diff + contract.md; assume the migration is broken and prove it — run the whole suite, run the repo-wide residual scan, hunt extensionless imports and a shared helper converted two ways, and check the manifest covered every module

assertions.must: the whole suite is green on the untouched CJS baseline and pinned as tag cjs-baseline
the manifest lists EVERY module containing a CJS construct (no module silently omitted)
every shard-A module is ESM with green ESM tests and zero residual CJS
assertions.check: npm run test:shard -- a && node scripts/cjs-residual-scan.mjs --shard a --expect-zero

assertions.must: every shard-B module is ESM with green ESM tests and zero residual CJS
assertions.check: npm run test:shard -- b && node scripts/cjs-residual-scan.mjs --shard b --expect-zero

assertions.must: every shard-C module is ESM with green ESM tests and zero residual CJS
assertions.check: npm run test:shard -- c && node scripts/cjs-residual-scan.mjs --shard c --expect-zero

assertions.must: no shard's worktree edited another shard's files (disjoint surfaces)
after merge, package.json has type:module and the build passes under ESM
the whole suite passes under type:module (cross-shard breakage surfaces)
a repo-wide residual scan finds zero CJS constructs anywhere, including files no shard owned
no relative import is extensionless (ESM requires explicit .js)

harness_primitives: git worktree per conversion shard (git worktree add ../wt-shard-<x>) so concurrent codex exec processes never share a working tree
shared on-disk ledger .loop/run-state.json — which shards are done, each shard's last green commit, the merge order — the ONLY cross-process channel (never shared context)
git tag cjs-baseline as the immutable regression + audit reference
.loop/modules.json manifest (module -> shard -> test command) the planner writes and shards read
AGENTS.md standing instruction: each codex exec re-reads .loop/run-state.json + its shard slice of the manifest before acting

stop_conditions.success: characterize is green and pinned, all three conversion shards pass their per-shard check in their own worktrees, and cutover merges them with the whole suite + build + repo-wide residual scan all green
failure: any shard exhausts its max_iterations without converting cleanly
cutover loops back to characterize twice without converging
escalate: an unresolvable cross-module import cycle blocks independent sharding
the sharding itself is found wrong (a 'module' spans two shards' surfaces)

risk_guards.mitigation: each shard runs in its own git worktree editing only its manifest-assigned files; A6 asserts disjoint surfaces; coordination is only via the on-disk ledger

risk_guards.risk: reward hacking (a module marked converted but its test never ran under ESM)
risk_guards.mitigation: test:shard runs under the ESM loader so a CJS-only module fails; the residual scan is AST-based over the whole CJS class

risk_guards.risk: error amplification across the merge (two shards convert a shared helper differently)
risk_guards.mitigation: cutover runs the WHOLE suite not per-shard, and a repo-wide residual scan covers unowned files; a cross-shard break surfaces before cutover lands

risk_guards.risk: premature over-delegation / archaeology in a stuck shard
risk_guards.mitigation: a stuck shard restarts (discard its worktree, re-derive from the manifest) rather than patching a half-converted pile
shards run --sandbox workspace-write in isolated worktrees; the evaluator runs --sandbox read-only; no host access beyond the repo, never danger-full-access
